summary: Added the Stage 11A stdlib-only read-only MCP tool registry and in-process dispatcher over existing project registry, planning, Story Loop, task, and worker-run services.

tests_run.command: uv run --no-sync python -B -m pytest tests/test_mcp_server.py -q -p no:cacheprovider
tests_run.summary: 5 passed; covered read-only tool listing, project-list delegation, plan/task/worker read delegation, validation errors, unknown tool errors, not-found errors, disabled MCP mode, and no worktree creation.
Planning integrity checks passed with Stage 11A claimed for implementation.
Story Loop status reported Stage 11A as the current running task before completion was recorded.
424 passed plus Ruff, format, mypy, CLI smoke checks, file justification, public hygiene, planning integrity, skill inventory, source inventory, protected locks, and uv lock --check.

A new MCP server module defines tool met.evidence: src/codex_supervisor/mcp_server.py defines McpToolDefinition, McpServerContext, read-only tool metadata payloads, JSON-schema-style input schemas, normalized success envelopes, and normalized error envelopes using only the standard library and existing repo modules.

Read-only handlers delegate to existing.evidence: Handlers call discover_projects, open_existing_planning_database(read_only=True), build_story_loop_status, list_plans, next_ready_afk_task, list_supervisor_tasks, and list_worker_runs directly; tests assert task status remains ready and no worktrees are created.

The dispatcher supports an explicit disa.evidence: McpServerContext(enabled=False) hides tool listings and dispatch_mcp_tool returns an mcp_disabled error envelope; full verify confirms CLI behavior remains green.

Focused tests cover tool listing, succes.evidence: tests/test_mcp_server.py covers read-only schemas, project-list success, plan/task/worker/status success, validation/unknown/not-found failures, disabled mode, and absence of worktree creation.

Planning SQLite and HANDOFF.md record th.evidence: plans/planning.sqlite3 contains the Stage 11A plan/task/claim rows, and HANDOFF.md records the shaped and implemented Stage 11A slice, verification commands, out-of-scope boundaries, and residual risks.

review.summary: Fresh-thread-style local review found one test-coverage gap for the plan_list success path; the focused test now exercises plan inspection. No open review findings remain after focused and full verification.

risks: Stage 11A intentionally implements an in-process read-only MCP contract, not a stdio or network MCP transport.
Mutating MCP tools for task enqueue, worker launch, review launch, artifact linking, insight updates, and skill-update proposals remain later Stage 11 slices.
Live Codex Exec launch remains unavailable in this Windows shell because the resolved WindowsApps codex.exe path returns Access is denied.
follow_up_tasks: Add the Stage 11B MCP stdio transport slice once the read-only dispatcher contract is accepted.
Add mutating MCP tools only after explicit schemas and queue mutation semantics are shaped in planning SQLite.
handoff_notes: Stage 11A establishes a tested read-only MCP tool contract without external dependencies, live worker launch, or transport packaging. Continue Stage 11 by shaping transport and mutating-tool slices through planning SQLite.
